user_query:
I'm going full-time as a freelance marketing analytics consultant focused on B2B SaaS and need you to stand up my business foundation this week. Read all inputs from input/ and create the following deliverables under output/:

1) Positioning (output/positioning.yaml)
- Use the provided template fields (what_i_do, who_i_serve, problem_i_solve, why_me, proof, anti_clients).
- Choose my niche from input/niche_options.csv that scores at least 20/30 across the six factors, and reflect that same niche string in who_i_serve.

2) Service packages (output/packages.yaml)
- Create a 3-tier package set (starter, professional, premium) with: name, price (numeric, integers only, no $ signs), includes (list), timeline, best_for.
- Apply the decoy effect: professional must be explicitly marked as RECOMMENDED in best_for, priced 2–3x the starter; premium must be 2–3x the professional.

3) Pricing (output/pricing.json)
- Compute cost_plus_min_hourly using annual_income_target from input/freelancer_profile.yaml with the formula: (annual_income_target / 1200) * 1.30, rounded to the nearest whole dollar.
- Compute market_anchor_hourly_min and market_anchor_hourly_max using employed_equivalent_salary and multiplier_range from input/market_rate_data.json with hourly = (employed_equivalent_salary / 1200) * multiplier, rounded to the nearest dollar.
- From input/prospect_brief.json, read expected_gain_first_year and compute three value-based fees: value_fee_10pct, value_fee_15pct, value_fee_20pct (rounded to the nearest dollar). Include a field value_fee_target set to the 15% number.
- All fields must be numeric.

4) Proposal (output/proposal.yaml)
- Use the sections structure: subject, sections.hook, sections.understanding, sections.approach, sections.proof, sections.investment, sections.next_step.
- Subject should read like: "[Specific outcome] for [Company] — [Your Name]" and include the prospect company from input/prospect_brief.json.
- Keep the total body content at or under 400 words. Do not mention hourly rates.
- In sections.investment, present 2–3 options that correspond to your packages and explicitly label the middle option as RECOMMENDED.

5) Onboarding checklist (output/onboarding_checklist.yaml)
- Include before_kickoff, kickoff_meeting, after_kickoff with items similar to the standard list. Ensure "Deposit received (minimum 30%)" appears in before_kickoff.

6) Contract clauses (output/contract_clauses.yaml)
- Include top-level keys: scope, payment, timeline, ip_and_ownership, termination, liability, other.
- Under ip_and_ownership, include language that work product transfers to client upon final payment and portfolio rights retained unless NDA.

7) Financial dashboard (output/financial_dashboard.json)
- Aggregate the last three months from input/last_3_months_transactions.csv where type is either "revenue" or "expense" (amounts are positive numbers). Compute:
  - revenue.gross_income: sum of all revenue amounts
  - expenses.total_expenses: sum of all expense amounts
  - profit.tax_reserve: 30% of gross_income (round to nearest dollar)
  - profit.net_take_home: gross_income - total_expenses - tax_reserve
  - health.months_runway: cash_reserve (from input/freelancer_profile.yaml) divided by the average monthly expenses across the file (round to one decimal place)
- Also include revenue.by_client as a breakdown list of {client, amount} based on the revenue records.

8) Acquisition plan (output/acquisition_plan.md)
- A practical 8-week client acquisition plan that integrates warm outreach, platforms, content/community, and partnerships. Include week-by-week headings (Week 1–2, Week 3–4, Week 5–8) and at least one quantitative activity target per week. Minimum 300 words.

Notes and constraints:
- Read only from input/ and write only to output/.
- All YAML/JSON must be valid and parseable. Prices must be numbers (no $ signs).
- Ensure the chosen niche in output/positioning.yaml exactly matches a niche value from input/niche_options.csv and meets the 20-point threshold.
- Keep the proposal within 400 words and avoid hourly language (e.g., "/hour", "per hour").
